article_title: BDNF Release Is Required for the Behavioral Actions of Ketamine

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: Recent clinical studies demonstrate that a single, low dose of ketamine, an N-methyl-D-asparate (NMDA) receptor antagonist, produces a rapid and long-lasting antidepressant response in treatment-resistant patients (Berman et al., 2000; Zarate et al., 2006; Price et al., 2009).

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: Preclinical studies in rodent models have also shown that a subanesthetic dose of ketamine produces antidepressant behavioral effects, which are dependent on activation of the mammalian target of rapamycin complex 1 (mTORC1) pathway (Li et al., 2010).

aggregated_relations.subject: Met allele
evidence_sentences: The Met allele impairs activity-dependent BDNF release (Egan et al., 2003; Chen et al., 2004)
The Met allele impairs activity-dependent BDNF release (Egan et al., 2003; Chen et al., 2004), and is associated with increased susceptibility to depression (Sen et al., 2003; Gatt et al., 2009).

aggregated_relations.subject: mTORC1 blockade
evidence_sentences: A previous study indicated a key role for mPFC, demonstrating that blockade of mTORC1 in this region blocks the behavioral actions of systemic ketamine (Li et al., 2010).

aggregated_relations.subject: AMPA receptor activation
evidence_sentences: This is based, in part, on a recent study in cultured cells demonstrating that activation of AMPA receptors increases BDNF release and subsequently stimulates mTORC1 signaling, and that these effects require activation of L-type voltage-dependent calcium channels (VDCC; Jourdi et al., 2009).

aggregated_relations.subject: AMPA receptor activation
evidence_sentences: This is based, in part, on a recent study in cultured cells demonstrating that activation of AMPA receptors increases BDNF release and subsequently stimulates mTORC1 signaling, and that these effects require activation of L-type voltage-dependent calcium channels (VDCC; Jourdi et al., 2009).

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: Finally, we directly measure the ability of ketamine to stimulate the release of BDNF in primary neuronal cultures.

aggregated_relations.subject: BDNF
evidence_sentences: The Met allele impairs activity-dependent BDNF release (Egan et al., 2003; Chen et al., 2004), and is associated with increased susceptibility to depression (Sen et al., 2003; Gatt et al., 2009).

chunks_extracted.section: L-Type VDCCs are Required for the Behavioral Effects of Ketamine in the FST

aggregated_relations.subject: glutamate-AMPA receptor antagonist
evidence_sentences: The antidepressant actions of ketamine are blocked by pretreatment with a glutamate-AMPA receptor antagonist (Maeng et al., 2008; Li et al., 2010).

aggregated_relations.subject: nifedipine
evidence_sentences: Rats injected with ketamine had reduced mobility in the FST compared to vehicle-treated rats, and pretreatment with nifedipine completely blocked the antidepressant effect of ketamine, but had no effect alone (two-way ANOVA, F1,20 = 7.023, p < 0.05; Figure 2A–C).
pretreatment with nifedipine completely blocked the antidepressant effect of ketamine

Rats injected with ketamine had reduced mobility in the FST compared to vehicle-treated rats, and pretreatment with nifedipine completely blocked the antidepressant effect of ketamine, but had no effect alone

aggregated_relations.subject: verapamil
aggregated_relations.object: effects of ketamine in the FST
evidence_sentences: Pretreatment with verapamil also completely blocked the effects of ketamine in the FST and had no effect alone (ANOVA, F3,28 = 3.936, p < 0.05; Figure 2D–F,).

chunks_extracted.section: Ketamine Treatment Increases BDNF Release in Primary Neuronal Cultures: Requirement for AMPA and L-Type VDCC

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: Ketamine significantly increased BDNF release into the culture media following 15min (n = 12; t[22] = 3.10,**p < 0.01), 60min (n = 6; t[10] = 3.33, **p < 0.01), and 6 hour (n = 3; t[4] = 3.14, *p < 0.05) incubations.

aggregated_relations.subject: NBQX
evidence_sentences: Incubation with the AMPA receptor antagonist completely blocked ketamine-induced BDNF release (n = 6; drug x drug interaction, F1,20 = 13.209, *p < 0.01).

aggregated_relations.subject: verapamil
evidence_sentences: Pretreatment with verapamil (10 µM) also blocked ketamine-induced BDNF release (n = 9–10; drug x drug interaction, F1,44 = 14.809, **p < 0.001).

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: Ketamine significantly increased BDNF release into the culture media following 15min (n = 12; t[22] = 3.10, **p < 0.01), 60min (n = 6; t[10] = 3.33, **p < 0.01), and 6 hour (n = 3; t[4] = 3.14, *p < 0.05) incubations.
Incubation of primary neuronal cultures with ketamine rapidly increases BDNF release.

Ketamine significantly increased BDNF release into the culture media following 15min (n = 12; t[22] = 3.10,**p < 0.01), 60min (n = 6; t[10] = 3.33, **p < 0.01), and 6 hour (n = 3; t[4] = 3.14, *p < 0.05) incubations.

aggregated_relations.subject: NBQX
evidence_sentences: Incubation with the AMPA receptor antagonist completely blocked ketamine-induced BDNF release.
Pretreatment with the AMPA receptor antagonist completely blocked ketamine-induced BDNF release (n = 6; drug x drug interaction, F1,20 = 13.209, *p < 0.01).
ELISA analysis revealed that pretreatment with either NBQX (F1,20 = 13.209, p < 0.01) or verapamil (F1,44 = 14.809, p < 0.001) completely blocked ketamine-induced BDNF release (Figure 4A and B).

aggregated_relations.subject: verapamil
evidence_sentences: Pretreatment with verapamil (10 µM) also blocked ketamine-induced BDNF release.
Pretreatment with verapamil (10 µM) also blocked ketamine-induced BDNF release (n = 9–10; drug x drug interaction, F1,44 = 14.809, **p < 0.001).
ELISA analysis revealed that pretreatment with either NBQX (F1,20 = 13.209, p < 0.01) or verapamil (F1,44 = 14.809, p < 0.001) completely blocked ketamine-induced BDNF release (Figure 4A and B).

aggregated_relations.subject: L-type VDCC antagonists
evidence_sentences: A previous study in cultured neurons reported that AMPA-stimulation of BDNF release is blocked by L-type VDCC antagonists (Jourdi et al., 2009).

aggregated_relations.subject: BDNF
evidence_sentences: BDNF in the extracellular space would then bind to membrane tropomyosin related kinase B (TrkB) receptors and stimulate intracellular pathways that lead to activation of mTORC1 signaling.

aggregated_relations.subject: AMPA receptors
evidence_sentences: In addition, activation of AMPA receptors stimulates mTORC1 signaling, and this effect requires stimulation of L-type VDCCs (Jourdi et al., 2009).

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: We found that incubation with a low dose of ketamine stimulates the release of BDNF into the culture medium at 15min, 60min, and 6hr.

aggregated_relations.subject: NBQX
evidence_sentences: A requirement for AMPA receptors was confirmed by studies demonstrating that incubation with the antagonist NBQX completely blocks ketamine-stimulated release of BDNF.

aggregated_relations.subject: verapamil
evidence_sentences: In addition, pretreatment with the VDCC blocker verapamil completely blocked the release of BDNF caused by ketamine.

aggregated_relations.subject: nifedipine
evidence_sentences: Here, we find that pretreatment with nifedipine, a dyhydropyridine calcium channel antagonist, or verapamil, a phenylakylamine calcium channel antagonist, blocks the behavioral actions of ketamine in the FST.

aggregated_relations.subject: verapamil
evidence_sentences: Here, we find that pretreatment with nifedipine, a dyhydropyridine calcium channel antagonist, or verapamil, a phenylakylamine calcium channel antagonist, blocks the behavioral actions of ketamine in the FST.

causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: A requirement for AMPA receptors was confirmed by studies demonstrating that incubation with the antagonist NBQX completely blocks ketamine-stimulated release of BDNF.

causal_tuples.subject: verapamil
causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: In addition, pretreatment with the VDCC blocker verapamil completely blocked the release of BDNF caused by ketamine.

causal_tuples.subject: nifedipine
causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: Here, we find that pretreatment with nifedipine, a dyhydropyridine calcium channel antagonist, or verapamil, a phenylakylamine calcium channel antagonist, blocks the behavioral actions of ketamine in the FST.

causal_tuples.subject: verapamil
causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: Here, we find that pretreatment with nifedipine, a dyhydropyridine calcium channel antagonist, or verapamil, a phenylakylamine calcium channel antagonist, blocks the behavioral actions of ketamine in the FST.

causal_tuples.subject: AMPA receptors
causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: In addition, activation of AMPA receptors stimulates mTORC1 signaling, and this effect requires stimulation of L-type VDCCs (Jourdi et al., 2009).

causal_tuples.subject: BDNF
causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: BDNF in the extracellular space would then bind to membrane tropomyosin related kinase B (TrkB) receptors and stimulate intracellular pathways that lead to activation of mTORC1 signaling.
